title: When Considering an Opinion or Advice: - Ask Yourself:
subtitle: Question Authority
description:
When considering an opinion or advice:
- Ask yourself: "Am I agreeing because of the person or because of the evidence?"
- Check the facts: Verify the information independently, regardless of who said it.
- Value expertise over titles: Focus on the actual knowledge or experience behind the opinion.

Example: A senior manager gives advice—analyze the content, not just their title.

faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Start by tracking how often you pause to evaluate claims. Set a target of three pauses per day and note your initial confidence level before each pause.

howTo.title: Practice the pause
howTo.summary: In your next conversations, ask yourself if you agree based on the person or the evidence. This small mental shift can help reduce automatic assent.

howTo.title: Verify claims independently
howTo.summary: After pausing, take a few minutes to verify the information presented. Use quick searches to check the validity of claims and gather supporting evidence.

howTo.title: Reflect on your findings
howTo.summary: Review your logged pauses and verifications. Analyze patterns in your decision-making and adjust your approach based on what you learn.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit of evaluating advice based on evidence. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/evaluate-advice-not-authority/.
